title: betula fruticosa var. gmelinii
intro: Betula fruticosa var. gmelinii, also known as Gmelin's birch, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the birch family (Betulaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Siberia and Manchuria. The bark and leaves of the Gmelin's birch contain medicinal compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Gmelin's birch can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
